stem: A crossing's automatic warning devices are NOT operating as required. May you occupy the crossing relying on them?
choices: No — the devices must be observed OPERATING for the minimum required time; if they aren't, provide manual protection
Yes, if you sound the whistle
answer: No — the devices must be observed OPERATING for the minimum required time; if they aren't, provide manual protection
explain: Rule 103.1 requires the devices be observed OPERATING for the minimum required time before occupying. If they aren't operating, you can't satisfy that — provide manual protection of the crossing.

stem: 'Minimum Required Time' refers to…
choices: how long the automatic warning devices must be observed operating before a movement occupies the crossing
how long the train blocks the road
the time to walk the crossing
answer: how long the automatic warning devices must be observed operating before a movement occupies the crossing
explain: Rule 103.1(a): Minimum Required Time = the period the automatic warning devices must be observed operating before the movement occupies the grade crossing.

stem: Approaching a gated crossing with a maximum allowable speed of 40 mph, you must observe the gates horizontal for at least…
answer: 5 seconds
explain: Above 15 mph, observe the gates in the horizontal position for at least 5 seconds before occupying.

stem: A fusee must NOT be placed…
choices: on a public crossing at grade, or where it may cause fire
within a mile of a station
answer: on a public crossing at grade, or where it may cause fire
explain: Rule 11: a fusee should not be placed on a public crossing at grade or where it may cause fire.

stem: The engine whistle/horn signal sounded when approaching a public crossing at grade is prescribed by…
answer: Rule 14
explain: Rule 14 (Engine Whistle Signals) prescribes the crossing whistle signal; 'engine whistle' also refers to 'engine horn.'

stem: Slow Track Protection (Rule 43) requires the request to be…
choices: in writing (when practicable on the prescribed form), with the speed restriction(s) and limits confirmed to the foreman in writing after GBO protection is provided
answer: in writing (when practicable on the prescribed form), with the speed restriction(s) and limits confirmed to the foreman in writing after GBO protection is provided
explain: Rule 43: the request must be in writing (when practicable on the prescribed form); after GBO protection, the speed restriction(s) and limits are confirmed to the foreman in writing.

stem: Planned Protection is Rule 42; Slow Track Protection is Rule…
explain: Planned Protection = Rule 42; Slow Track Protection = Rule 43 (each paired with an 800-series equivalent, e.g. 842/843).

stem: Rule 41 (protection of track work on non-main track and in cautionary limits) does NOT apply on…
choices: main tracks outside cautionary limits, signalled sidings, or other signalled tracks
answer: main tracks outside cautionary limits, signalled sidings, or other signalled tracks
explain: Rule 41 is not applicable on main tracks outside of cautionary limits, signalled sidings and other signalled tracks, or tracks specified in special instructions.

stem: A foreman protecting track work or operating track units holds a…
answer: TOP (Track Occupancy Permit)
explain: A TOP (Track Occupancy Permit) is the authority a foreman holds for the protection of track work and operation of track units.

stem: In OCS, to enter limits where a foreman holds protection, a movement must first be…
choices: restricted by its clearance to protect against the foreman (Rule 311)
answer: restricted by its clearance to protect against the foreman (Rule 311)
explain: Rule 311 (OCS): a movement is restricted by its clearance to protect against a foreman's working limits — you don't enter without that protection.

stem: Special instructions specify when Rules 42/842 and 43/843 apply on non-main track. The '8xx' versions (842/843) are…
choices: the track-unit / TOP equivalents of the planned- and slow-track-protection rules
answer: the track-unit / TOP equivalents of the planned- and slow-track-protection rules
explain: Rule 40: 42/842 and 43/843 are paired — the 800-series are the track-unit/TOP equivalents of Planned Protection (42) and Slow Track Protection (43).

stem: For Planned Protection, who arranges the display of the prescribed flags?
choices: the foreman named in the GBO
answer: the foreman named in the GBO
explain: Rule 42(a): once protection is provided and the limits are confirmed in writing, the foreman named in the GBO arranges for the display of the prescribed flags.
